---
title: "Sodium Management"
---

Sodium Management
Sodium Repletion
• Na check q4h (ICU) or q4-6h (floor • Calculate sodium deficit = 0.6x body weight x (desired [Na] –current [Na])
• Target repletion rate: 6 mEq/L/day • Use 0.5for women/elderly men or 0.45for elderly women
Estimate of 1L of any saline infusion on serum sodium DI SIADH CSW
Change in serum sodium = [infusion sodium concentration (mmol/L) –serum Urine output Increased Decreased Increased
sodium concentration (mmol/L)] / (total body water + 1) Serum Na High Low Low
Infusion Sodium concentration (mmol/L) Urine Na Low High High
5% NaClin water 855
3% NaClin water 513 Serum Osm High Low Normal/Low
0.9% NaClin water 154 Urine Osm Low High Normal/Low
Lactate Ringer 130 CVP Normal/Low High Low
0.45% NaClin water 77 ADH Low High Normal
0.2% NaClin D5W 34
D5W 0
Practical Approach to Hyponatremia (uptodate)
Practical Low serum sodium (<135 mEq/L)
A H p y p p r o o n a a c t h re t m o ia Hyperglycemia present? * m A g d / d d L 2 o m f E s q er / u L m t o g l t u h c e o s s e e r a u b m o v so e d th iu e m n o fo rm r e a v l e v r a y l u 1 e 00
(uptodate) No Calculate co Y r e r s ected sodium* Normal H ind yp u e c r e g d l y h c y e p m o i n a a tremia
Low
Could extra solutes be present in serum:
- Is ptpostop after prostate or uterine surgery?
- Has the ptreceived IV mannitol or IVIG
Reason to s N us o pect pseudohyponatremia (due to Yes I - so T o U sm RP ol a an r d o r h y h s y t p er e o r s o c s o m py o l c a a r n h b y e p a o s n s a o t c r i e at m ed ia :
h - yp I e s r s li e p r i u d m em li i p a e o m r i h c y ? perproteinemia): YesM ser e u a m su re N or o h r i m gh al w (g i l t y h c i a n b e s , o s r o p r t b io it n o l) of irrigation solutions
- Is ptjaundiced? osmolality - If suspected pseudohyponatremia, have
- Is there history of plasma cell dyscrasi N a? o L (< o 2 w 80 mosmol/kg) s If o d n i o u r m m m al, e a m su ea re su d r b e y t o d t i a r l e c p t r o p t o e t i e n n a ti n o d m li e p tr id y. s .
Hypotonic hyponatremia
Use of thiazide diuretic? NoGFR substantially impaired?YesRenal failure
Yes
Stop thiazide
No
Hyponatremia resolved?YesThiazide induced hyponatremia
No
Edema? (peripheral or pulmonary edema, or ascites)YesLow effective arterial blood volume (heart failure, cirrhosis)
M an e d a s u u ri r n e e u o ri s n m e o s l o a d li i t u y m NoSigns of hypovolem Y ia es (low BP, orthostatic)? YesMeasure urine sodium L (< o 2 w 5 m Eq/L) H Ex y t p ra o r v e o n l a e l m lo i s c s e h s y ( p G o I n , a th tr i e rd m s i p a ace, diuretics)
Urine osmolality <100?YesU AF ri T ne E o R s m th o e l r a a l p it y y i m ni e ti a a s t u e r d e ? d YesR - ec M ov il e d r y h f y r p o o m v o o l n e e m o i f a f ( o gi l v lo e w n ing: (25-40 mEq/L) I R n e f m us e e a s is u o re to s n e i r c u s m al i s n o e d ( i e u x m . 1L over 1hr)
No Urine N s o odium >40? Rapid wat N er o consumption? - i H g s l o u y t c p o o o n c p i o c i r t f t u l i u c i o i t d a id s r ) i s s ) m(given H (> i 4 g 0 h m Eq/L) H Re y n p a o l v l o o l s e s m es ic hyponatremia
E
m
n
E
s
q
ur
o
e
v
s
e
o
r
d
n
i
e
u
x
m
t 2
in
t a
h
k
r
e
s (
>
ex
.
L
NoH
pr
i
o
g
t
h
ei
f
n
l u
d
i
i
d
e
,
t :
low
Review hx and meds
isotonic saline over 1 or more hrs) - “Beer potomania” AM cortisol and ACTH stim test
Yes - “Tea and toast diet”
Remeasure urine osmolality Low cortisol Using Head injury
and sodium YesS
in
e
t
l
o
f
x
i
i
n
c
d
a
u
ti
c
o
e
n
d
:
water Positive ACTH stim test diuretics Cranial surgery
U ur r i i n n e e o N sm a < < 4 1 0 0 0 or U ur r in in e e o N sm a > > 4 1 0 0 o 0 r - - P E s n y d c u h r o a s n is ce event P in r s im uf a fi r c y i e a n d c r y e nal D in i d u u r c e e ti d c C sa e l r t ebral
H hy y p p o o n v a o t l r e e m m i i c a E de v f a ic lu ie a n te c y f o a r n g d l u h c y o p c o o th rt y ic ro o i i d d i sm - Ecstasy use ( m gl i u n c e o ra c l o o r c t o ic r o ti i c d o a id n d wasting
deficiency)
AM cortisol and ACTH stim test abnormal?YesGlucocorticoid
deficiency
No
TSH very elevated? YesSevere hypothyroidism
No
SIADH or nephrogenic SIADHevaluate underlying etiology
